Drive on Sunshine

Pair your charger with solar panels.

With solar, you generate the electricity your EV consumes — effectively driving on free fuel. A typical Alberta solar system produces 10,000–12,000 kWh/year. Your EV uses 3,000–4,500 kWh/year. That means your solar powers your car AND your home.

FAQ

Common questions.

Wallbox Pulsar Plus charger, dedicated 240V circuit (up to 10m wire), all electrical work, permitting, city inspection, app setup, and personal walkthrough. Panel upgrades or extended wiring quoted separately during free assessment.
Yes. Compatible with every EV and plug-in hybrid — Tesla, Hyundai, Kia, Ford, Chevy, VW, BMW, Rivian, Lucid, and all others. Comes with J1772 and NACS (Tesla) connectors.
Both charge any EV overnight. 40A: up to 40 km/hr, plug-in or hardwired. 48A: up to 50 km/hr (20% faster), hardwired. Go 48A if you have a large battery EV or want future-proofing.
$0 down, 0% interest for 12 months through Financeit. Longer terms also available. Get pre-qualified in 2 minutes →
Not always. Most newer homes (200A panel) handle it fine. If your panel is near capacity, the Wallbox Power Boost (+$699) manages load without an upgrade. We assess everything for free.
Yes. The Wallbox supports Eco-Smart solar charging. A typical Alberta solar system produces 10,000–12,000 kWh/year; a typical EV uses 3,000–4,500 kWh/year. Your solar powers your car AND your home. Learn more →
Yes. NEMA Type 4 rated. Operates from −30°C to 40°C — built for Edmonton winters.
